name: Kennedy v. Plan Administrator for DuPont Sav. and Investment Plan
question: Does the Employee Retirement Income Security Act's anti-alienation provision prevent the enforcement of a waiver recognized by federal common law which holds that a spouse's right to the other spouse's pension benefits is rendered void upon divorce?
description: A case in which the Court held that the Employee Retirement Income Security Act's anti-alienation provision nonetheless recognizes a wavier which holds that a spouse's right to the other spouse's pension benefits is void upon divorce.
